turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="gSky" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#87CEEB"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#B9E0FF"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="gField" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#9DC95A"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#6EA02E"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="gSoil" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#D9A24A"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#C07A2E"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- Sky -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="300" fill="url(#gSky)" />
  <!-- Sun -->
  <circle cx="420" cy="80" r="44" fill="#FFD54F" />

  <!-- Rolling hills -->
  <path d="M0 260 C120 200 220 220 320 200 C420 180 520 230 512 300 L512 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#7DB75B" />
  <path d="M0 300 C120 260 220 280 320 260 C420 240 520 290 512 360 L512 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#6EA02E" opacity="0.9" />

  <!-- Plowed field foreground with rows -->
  <rect x="0" y="320" width="512" height="192" fill="url(#gField)" />
  <g stroke="#8B5A2B" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M-20 380 C90 370 170 390 270 380 C370 370 450 390 540 380" />
    <path d="M-20 420 C90 410 170 430 270 420 C370 410 450 430 540 420" />
    <path d="M-20 460 C90 450 170 470 270 460 C370 450 450 470 540 460" />
  </g>

  <!-- Barn -->
  <g transform="translate(60,220)">
    <rect x="0" y="30" width="140" height="120" fill="#C73B3B" stroke="#8C2B2B" stroke-width="4" rx="4" />
    <polygon points="-10,30 70, -30 150,30" fill="#9B2F2F" stroke="#7A2323" stroke-width="3" />
    <rect x="50" y="80" width="40" height="70" fill="#5C2A1F" stroke="#412014" stroke-width="3" />
    <rect x="18" y="58" width="26" height="22" fill="#FFDFA6" opacity="0.9" stroke="#7A4F2E" stroke-width="2" />
  </g>

  <!-- Hay bale (rolled) -->
  <g transform="translate(360,360)">
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="52" ry="40" fill="#E6B55B" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="6" />
    <ellipse cx="-4" cy="-2" rx="38" ry="28" fill="none" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="4" />
    <ellipse cx="-8" cy="0" rx="18" ry="12" fill="none" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="3" />
  </g>

  <!-- Foreground wheat stalks -->
  <g stroke="#8C5A1A" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round">
    <!-- stalk 1 -->
    <path d="M110 420 C110 380 120 360 130 340" />
    <g fill="#F2C15A" stroke="#B8862B" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="134" cy="344" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(20 134 344)" />
      <ellipse cx="126" cy="356" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(10 126 356)" />
      <ellipse cx="118" cy="368" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(-5 118 368)" />
      <ellipse cx="110" cy="380" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(-20 110 380)" />
    </g>
    <!-- stalk 2 -->
    <path d="M150 430 C150 390 160 370 170 350" />
    <g fill="#F2C15A" stroke="#B8862B" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="174" cy="354" rx="7" ry="10" transform="rotate(18 174 354)" />
      <ellipse cx="166" cy="366" rx="7" ry="10" transform="rotate(8 166 366)" />
      <ellipse cx="158" cy="378" rx="7" ry="10" transform="rotate(-6 158 378)" />
    </g>
    <!-- stalk 3 -->
    <path d="M200 440 C200 400 210 380 220 360" />
    <g fill="#F2C15A" stroke="#B8862B" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="224" cy="364" rx="6" ry="9" transform="rotate(16 224 364)" />
      <ellipse cx="216" cy="376" rx="6" ry="9" transform="rotate(4 216 376)" />
      <ellipse cx="208" cy="388" rx="6" ry="9" transform="rotate(-12 208 388)" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Scattered grain/harvest accents -->
  <g fill="#F0C36A" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="1">
    <ellipse cx="300" cy="430" rx="10" ry="6" transform="rotate(-15 300 430)" />
    <ellipse cx="260" cy="450" rx="12" ry="7" transform="rotate(8 260 450)" />
    <ellipse cx="340" cy="470" rx="9" ry="5" transform="rotate(-10 340 470)" />
  </g>

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{"svg":"<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="gSky" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#87CEEB"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#B9E0FF"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="gField" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#9DC95A"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#6EA02E"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="gSoil" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#D9A24A"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#C07A2E"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- Sky -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="300" fill="url(#gSky)" />
  <!-- Sun -->
  <circle cx="420" cy="80" r="44" fill="#FFD54F" />

  <!-- Rolling hills -->
  <path d="M0 260 C120 200 220 220 320 200 C420 180 520 230 512 300 L512 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#7DB75B" />
  <path d="M0 300 C120 260 220 280 320 260 C420 240 520 290 512 360 L512 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#6EA02E" opacity="0.9" />

  <!-- Plowed field foreground with rows -->
  <rect x="0" y="320" width="512" height="192" fill="url(#gField)" />
  <g stroke="#8B5A2B" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M-20 380 C90 370 170 390 270 380 C370 370 450 390 540 380" />
    <path d="M-20 420 C90 410 170 430 270 420 C370 410 450 430 540 420" />
    <path d="M-20 460 C90 450 170 470 270 460 C370 450 450 470 540 460" />
  </g>

  <!-- Barn -->
  <g transform="translate(60,220)">
    <rect x="0" y="30" width="140" height="120" fill="#C73B3B" stroke="#8C2B2B" stroke-width="4" rx="4" />
    <polygon points="-10,30 70, -30 150,30" fill="#9B2F2F" stroke="#7A2323" stroke-width="3" />
    <rect x="50" y="80" width="40" height="70" fill="#5C2A1F" stroke="#412014" stroke-width="3" />
    <rect x="18" y="58" width="26" height="22" fill="#FFDFA6" opacity="0.9" stroke="#7A4F2E" stroke-width="2" />
  </g>

  <!-- Hay bale (rolled) -->
  <g transform="translate(360,360)">
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="52" ry="40" fill="#E6B55B" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="6" />
    <ellipse cx="-4" cy="-2" rx="38" ry="28" fill="none" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="4" />
    <ellipse cx="-8" cy="0" rx="18" ry="12" fill="none" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="3" />
  </g>

  <!-- Foreground wheat stalks -->
  <g stroke="#8C5A1A" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round">
    <!-- stalk 1 -->
    <path d="M110 420 C110 380 120 360 130 340" />
    <g fill="#F2C15A" stroke="#B8862B" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="134" cy="344" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(20 134 344)" />
      <ellipse cx="126" cy="356" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(10 126 356)" />
      <ellipse cx="118" cy="368" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(-5 118 368)" />
      <ellipse cx="110" cy="380" rx="8" ry="12" transform="rotate(-20 110 380)" />
    </g>
    <!-- stalk 2 -->
    <path d="M150 430 C150 390 160 370 170 350" />
    <g fill="#F2C15A" stroke="#B8862B" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="174" cy="354" rx="7" ry="10" transform="rotate(18 174 354)" />
      <ellipse cx="166" cy="366" rx="7" ry="10" transform="rotate(8 166 366)" />
      <ellipse cx="158" cy="378" rx="7" ry="10" transform="rotate(-6 158 378)" />
    </g>
    <!-- stalk 3 -->
    <path d="M200 440 C200 400 210 380 220 360" />
    <g fill="#F2C15A" stroke="#B8862B" stroke-width="1">
      <ellipse cx="224" cy="364" rx="6" ry="9" transform="rotate(16 224 364)" />
      <ellipse cx="216" cy="376" rx="6" ry="9" transform="rotate(4 216 376)" />
      <ellipse cx="208" cy="388" rx="6" ry="9" transform="rotate(-12 208 388)" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Scattered grain/harvest accents -->
  <g fill="#F0C36A" stroke="#C28B38" stroke-width="1">
    <ellipse cx="300" cy="430" rx="10" ry="6" transform="rotate(-15 300 430)" />
    <ellipse cx="260" cy="450" rx="12" ry="7" transform="rotate(8 260 450)" />
    <ellipse cx="340" cy="470" rx="9" ry="5" transform="rotate(-10 340 470)" />
  </g>

</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG for Wheat**

I'm working on creating an SVG representation of wheat. I'll use positions along the bottom and plot lines representing the stalks, with small ovals for grains along a curved path. I want to keep it simple by using path strokes and ellipses without any mapping loops since it'll be static. I need to ensure that I format the output correctly in JSON, paying attention to escaping double quotes and allowing for newlines. Let’s get started on crafting the SVG content!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

**Generating one-word guesses**

I need to provide 20 one-word guesses based on an image of a farm field that features a red barn, hay bale, sun, wheat stalks, and hills. Possible key words include "barn," "hay," and "wheat." Barn seems to be the most prominent, so I'll put that first, followed by "hay" and "bale." I also think "haybale" could be a valid entry since many puzzles accept it as a compound word. I need to ensure all guesses are acceptable single words without spaces.**Creating unique guesses**

I see there’s a small building in the image, likely a barn. I need to generate a list of 20 unique one-word guesses related to a farm setting. Words like "barn," "hay," "bale," "farm," and "wheat" come to mind. I think "countryside" and "pasture" are good options too. Each word needs to be a valid single word, in lowercase, without any punctuation or spaces, and I’ll aim to avoid repeating roots. Let's craft the list in order of confidence!**Preparing JSON format**

I've gathered my list of words related to the farm setting: barn, hay, bale, haybale, farm, farmhouse, wheat, grain, harvest, field, barnyard, countryside, rural, straw, silo, pasture, crop, sun, tractor, and fence.
